turns.user_message: I used to bounce back from small mistakes without giving them a second thought, but lately I just sit with the heavy feeling until it settles in my chest. It has been dragging on for months now, and I still catch myself replaying every awkward conversation in my head. I know I am overthinking it, yet the embarrassment feels completely real and impossible to shake off. I just wish I could return to that lighter version of myself who never carried this much weight around.

I used to laugh off my own slip-ups, but now I spend hours dissecting every little thing I said wrong. This pattern has been sticking with me for so long that it feels like my default setting rather than a passing phase. I keep trying to convince myself that nobody else notices, but the quiet shame just keeps piling up inside me anyway. It is exhausting to constantly monitor my own behavior just to avoid feeling like a complete disappointment.

I used to move through my days with a quiet confidence, but lately I feel like I am constantly apologizing for my own existence. This heavy sense of regret has been following me around for years, and it has completely warped how I see myself. Every single interaction leaves me convinced that I am fundamentally flawed and unworthy of basic respect. I hate that I let this lingering guilt dictate everything I do, because I know it is slowly eating away at who I actually am.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by contrasting with how things used to be. This has been going on for a while.
